steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a dinosaur fossil specimen's nickname — the answer must name a specific nickname, not merely note that nicknames exist.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Nicknames are given to many dinosaur fossil specimens, including one housed at a museum in Houston, Texas.': mentions a nicknamed specimen but never names the nickname. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at museums all over the world, and many of these specimens are given nicknames.': only restates that nicknames exist — no specific nickname given. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Wyrex is the nickname of a Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen housed at the Houston Museum of Natural Science in Houston, Texas.': directly names the nickname 'Wyrex' and identifies the specimen, providing a concrete example. ✓
  • Choice D — 'A Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period ... is housed at the Houston Museum of Natural Science.': describes the specimen but omits its nickname entirely. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
